title: Magyar Announces Government to Take Power in Early May; Demands President Resign
detail: Péter Magyar announced that Hungary's new government could assume power at the beginning of May following President Tamás Sulyok's assurance that Magyar would lead government formation. Magyar set a constitutional deadline of May 12 for the new parliament to elect a prime minister. He called on heads of state-controlled institutions — including public broadcaster MTVA — to resign, vowing sweeping anti-corruption reforms and Hungary's full reintegration into EU and NATO frameworks.

media.caption: Magyar announces new government to take power at beginning of May
